In feedback almost all employees said that volunteering improved their
Last year some staff helped unemployed people with their

Where will the Digital Inclusion Day be held?
options: A. at the company’s training facility

contentBlocks.prompt: What should staff do if they want to take part in the Digital Inclusion Day?
options: C. get permission from their manager

contentBlocks.prompt: What TWO things are mentioned about the participants on the last Digital Inclusion Day?
options: B. They never used their computer.
C. Their phones were mostly old-fashioned.
D. They only used their phones for making calls.
E. They initially showed little interest.

parts.title: Part 3
parts.content:
Questions 21-25

Choose the correct letter, A , B or C . Planning a presentation on nanotechnology

Questions 26-30

What comments do the speakers make about each of the following aspects of Russ’s previous presentation? Choose FIVE answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-G , next to Questions. Comments Aspects of Russ’s previous presentation

contentBlocks.text:
Questions 21-25

Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

Planning a presentation on nanotechnology

contentBlocks.prompt: Russ says that his difficulty in planning the presentation is due to
options: A. his lack of knowledge about the topic.
B. his uncertainly about what he should try to achieve.
C. the short time that he has for preparation.

contentBlocks.prompt: Russ and his tutor agree that his approach in the presentation will be
options: A. to concentrate on how nanotechnology is used in one field.
B. to follow the chronological development of nanotechnology.
C. to show the range of applications of nanotechnology.

contentBlocks.prompt: In connection with slides, the tutor advises Russ to
options: A. talk about things that he can find slides to illustrate.
B. look for slides to illustrate the points he makes.

contentBlocks.prompt: They both agree that the best way for Russ to start his presentation is
options: A. to encourage the audience to talk.
B. to explain what Russ intends to do.

contentBlocks.prompt: What does the tutor advise Russ to do next while preparing his presentation?
options: A. summarise the main point he wants to make
B. read the notes he has already made
C. list the topics he wants to cover

contentBlocks.text:
Questions 26-30

What comments do the speakers make about each of the following aspects of Russ’s previous presentation?

Choose FIVE answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-G, next to Questions.

Comments

Aspects of Russ’s previous presentation

parts.title: Part 4
parts.content:
Questions 31-40

Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer. Episodic memory the ability to recall details, e.g. the time and [31] of past events different to semantic memory – the ability to remember general information about the [32] , which does not involve recalling [33] information Forming episodic memories involves three steps: Encoding involves receiving and processing information the more [34] Given to an event, the more successfully it can be encoded to remember a [35] , it is useful to have a strategy for encoding such information Consolidation how memories are strengthened and stored most effective when memories can be added to a [36] Of related information the [37] Of retrieval affects the strength of memories Retrieval memory retrieval often depends on using a prompt, e.g. the [38] Of an object near to the place where you left your car Episodic memory impairments these affect people with a wide range of medical conditions games which stimulate the [39] have been found to help people with schizophrenia children with autism may have difficulty forming episodic memories – possibly because their concept of the [40] may be absent memory training may help autistic children develop social skills
